task: Build Song Time activity with Howler.js audio, lyric sync, and nursery rhymes seed
approach_rationale: Follow exact patterns established by ListenAndFind and MatchingPairs: page.tsx loads data, passes to component, component splits into focused sub-components. Reuse existing hooks (useDB, useAudio), existing DB store (songs), and Tailwind theme tokens. Minimal type changes (add playCount? to Song). No new stores or infrastructure needed.

files_to_modify.reason: Add playCount?: number to Song interface so play counts can be persisted in the songs IndexedDB store.

Replace placeholder 1-song seed with 3 full nursery rhymes (Twinkle, Old MacDonald, Head Shoulders) including multi-line lyrics, Ms timestamps, and highlightWordIds referencing existing vocabulary IDs.
Add getAllSongs() and putSong() helper functions. The songs store already exists and is seeded; these functions are the missing read/write API analogous to getAllWords/putWord.
Add seek(), pause(), and duration() methods to the Howl mock class. SongPlayer calls howl.seek() in the RAF sync loop and howl.pause() on word-tap; tests will fail without these methods.
Replace 'Coming soon' placeholder with a full client-side page that loads songs + vocabulary from IndexedDB and renders <SongTime>.

files_to_create.purpose: Top-level orchestrator. Manages screen state (picker | player), local play-count map (initialised from song.playCount), handles song-end DB write. ~80 lines.
Song tile grid. Shows cover image (next/image with placeholder fallback), EN + ZH title, play-count badge. Tapping a tile calls onSelect(song). ~80 lines.

Player screen. Creates Howl instance on mount, listens for loaderror → sets audioError. RAF loop reads howl.seek() while playing, finds currentLineIndex. Play/pause toggle, word-chip interaction (pause→playWord→resume), song-end banner. ~145 lines.
Tappable vocabulary chip showing EN+ZH words. Scales up + lavender border when active. Calls onTap() on press. ~40 lines.

Jest tests covering: renders song-time, song-picker visible by default, tile click transitions to song-player, audio error state renders (no crash), play button present, back button returns to picker, song-end completion banner appears, word chip tap feedback.

steps: 1. Add playCount?: number to Song interface in src/types/index.ts
2. Replace songs-seed.ts with 3 nursery rhymes having full lyrics, Ms timestamps, and valid highlightWordIds
3. Add getAllSongs() and putSong() to db.ts
4. Add seek(), pause(), duration() to Howl mock in __mocks__/howler.ts
6. Create SongPicker.tsx (~80 lines) with cover tiles, EN+ZH, play-count badges
7. Create SongPlayer.tsx (~145 lines) with Howler setup, RAF sync, lyric display, word chip handlers, song-end banner
8. Create SongTime.tsx (~80 lines) as picker/player orchestrator
9. Replace song-time/page.tsx with client page that loads songs+vocabulary, renders SongTime
10. Write SongTime.test.tsx covering all required scenarios
11. Run npm test to verify all tests pass
expected_tests: tapping a song tile shows data-testid=song-player
audio error state renders friendly message and Retry button (no crash)
back button in player returns to song-picker
song completion banner shows Listen Again and Choose Another buttons
word chip renders with EN+ZH text and data-testid=word-chip
word chip tap triggers visual feedback (active class)

risk_areas.risk: RAF is not properly cycled in jsdom; the lyric sync loop may never fire during tests
risk_areas.mitigation: Mock window.requestAnimationFrame in tests to either be a no-op or call callback once, and test lyric sync by directly setting currentLineIndex state via props/state manipulation

risk_areas.risk: Testing the audio error path requires manually emitting a loaderror event on the Howl mock
risk_areas.mitigation: The existing Howl mock has emit() method; tests can reach into the Howl instance via a mock factory pattern and call emit('loaderror')

risk_areas.risk: Users who already have littlebridge v1 in their browser will have the old 'Animal Friends' song, not the new 3 songs
risk_areas.mitigation: Acceptable for development. Comment in db.ts explains seed is applied on fresh DB only. Production release would bump DB version.

risk_areas.risk: All 3 MP3 files don't exist; Howler fires loaderror immediately
risk_areas.mitigation: SongPlayer catches loaderror → sets audioError=true → renders friendly '🎵 Song loading...' error UI with Retry button. No crash.

risk_areas.area: playCount field absent in existing seeded songs
risk_areas.risk: db.get('songs', id) returns Song without playCount; display would be undefined
risk_areas.mitigation: All display sites use (song.playCount ?? 0); putSong spreads and adds playCount.

persistence_strategy.songs: IndexedDB songs store, seeded on DB version 1 creation. Read via getAllSongs(). Song.playCount updated via putSong() at song-end event.
persistence_strategy.vocabulary: IndexedDB vocabulary store (existing). Read via getAllWords() in page.tsx for word chip display.
persistence_strategy.play_counts: Stored as song.playCount?: number on the Song record in IndexedDB songs store. Updated with putSong(db, {...song, playCount: (song.playCount ?? 0) + 1}).

error_handling_approach.db_load_failure: page.tsx wraps getAllSongs/getAllWords in try/catch; sets error=true; renders bilingual error message + Go Home button (same pattern as listen-find/page.tsx)
error_handling_approach.audio_load_failure: SongPlayer attaches onloaderror handler to Howl instance; sets audioError=true; renders '🎵 Song unavailable / 歌曲暂时无法播放' with Retry button. Retry re-creates the Howl instance.
error_handling_approach.empty_songs: SongPicker shows empty state if songs array is empty (defensive; should never happen with seed)
